item.title: 5-day preliminary hearing for Charlie Kirk's alleged killer starts Monday
item.description: Prosecutors will present their case this week against Tyler Robinson, the man accused of killing conservative activist Charlie Kirk.

item.title: China test-launches ballistic missile from sub in South Pacific, draws protests
item.description: China's military test-launched a long-range ballistic missile from one a nuclear-powered submarine in the South Pacific, drawing protest and concern from countries in the region.

item.title: 12-hour funeral procession for slain leader through streets of Tehran underway
item.description: An estimated 15 to 20 million people, along with foreign dignitaries and religious leaders, were expected to take part in the ceremonies, which will last for several days.

item.title: At least 10 killed in large-scale attack on Kyiv day before NATO summit begins
item.description: The attacks, which involved missiles and drones, caused a residential building in Ukraine's capital to partially collapse.

item.title: The 2026 FIFA World Cup schedule and how to watch
item.description: With 104 World Cup games being played in the U.S., Canada and Mexico, it's like "a Super Bowl every single day for five weeks," U.S. team captain Tim Ream told CBS News.

item.title: Delta Air Lines flight hit by firework while landing at Midway International Airport
item.description: A Delta Air Lines flight landing in Chicago was hit by a firework as it was coming to land at Midway International Airport on Saturday.

item.title: Seaplane carrying passengers makes hard landing in East River, FDNY says
item.description: A seaplane carrying at least eight people made a hard landing in New York City's East River on Sunday, officials said.

item.title: Balogun's red card suspension lifted after Trump called FIFA chief, sources say
item.description: Balogun's reinstatement came after President Trump called FIFA President Gianni Infantino last week to ask whether the organization was going to review the situation, sources told CBS News.

item.title: Annin Flagmakers: The oldest, largest flagmaker in the U.S.
item.description: Jericka Duncan visits Annin Flagmakers' factory in South Boston, Virginia.

item.title: Money for repairs to national parks going to D.C. beautification projects
item.description: About 400 sites managed by the National Park Service are facing a maintenance backlog estimated at more than $24 billion, but the money aimed for repairs is being diverted. The Washington Post reports the Trump administration has used at least $90 million from national park entry fees to help pay for beautification efforts in the nation's capital ahead of the America 250 celebration. Caitlin Huey-Burns reports.

item.title: Critical hearing this week for suspect in Charlie Kirk murder
item.description: Tyler Robinson, the man accused of assassinating Charlie Kirk, will be in court for a major hearing this week. Jonah Kaplan reports.

item.title: Funeral underway for Ayatollah Ali Khamenei
item.description: In Iran this weekend, millions took part in public funeral services for the country's slain supreme leader, Ayatollah Ali Khamenei, and called for revenge for his death in a U.S. and Israeli airstrike. Leigh Kiniry has more.

item.title: Trump attacks Democrats in 4th of July speech marking 250 years of America
item.description: President Trump's speech marking America's milestone founding stressed patriotism and partisanship. He branded Democrats as communists after a series of victories by democratic socialist candidates across the country. Taurean Small reports.

item.title: Millions brace for flooding after deadly heat, storms damper July 4th celebrations
item.description: While millions of Americans across the Northeast experienced record-setting temperatures, thunderstorms in the Midwest downed trees, ruptured power lines and made transportation treacherous.

item.title: Extreme weather mars 4th of July weekend; seaplane makes hard landing in NYC river
item.description: A number of cities broke temperature records over the Fourth of July weekend. In storm-ravaged New Jersey, authorities say at least 19 deaths are blamed on the heat. Meanwhile, a seaplane was forced to make a hard landing in New York City's East River on Sunday. Shanelle Kaul reports on it all and meteorologist Andrew Kozak has a look at the forecast.

item.title: Folarin Balogun will play in U.S. World Cup match against Belgium after FIFA suspends match ban
item.description: Folarin Balogun will play in the U.S.-Belgium World Cup match after FIFA suspended his one-game ban following a red card. Nicole Valdes reports.

item.title: Mallory McMorrow suspends Senate campaign in Michigan
item.description: State Sen. Mallory McMorrow announced on Sunday that she is suspending her campaign for Senate, narrowing the Democratic field ahead of the competitive August primary.

item.title: How the Savannah Bananas celebrate foster families
item.description: The exhibition baseball league Banana Ball focuses on entertaining people of all ages – including "Bananas Foster," a nonprofit that honors foster families.

item.title: Anderson Cooper's farewell to 60 Minutes
item.description: In a heartfelt sign-off, Cooper reflects on the stories, risks, adventures, and human connections that defined his two decades with television's most iconic newsmagazine.

item.title: America's untapped rare earth source: Its trash
item.description: One untapped resource to meet the rising need for rare earth elements: recycling what's already been used.

item.title: Bringing wackiness to baseball has been a home run for the Savannah Bananas
item.description: Savannah Bananas owner Jesse Cole dreamed of making baseball livelier and more fun. Now the team is taking its dances, acrobatics, and trick plays to sold-out stadiums across the U.S.

item.title: London's cabbies memorize 25,000 streets. But will robotaxis overtake them?
item.description: London black cab drivers, who are required to memorize thousands of streets to get their license, are being tested in a new way. Several companies are trying to bring robotaxis to the city's streets.

item.title: Pentagon works with U.S. rare earths company to counter China's dominance
item.description: The Trump administration is working with a U.S. company to challenge China's dominance over rare earth elements. The metals are essential for components in smartphones, robotics, fighter jets, and drones.

item.title: Savannah Bananas pack stadiums with their zany twist on baseball | 60 Minutes
item.description: Savannah Bananas owner Jesse Cole dreamed of making baseball livelier and more fun. Now the team is taking its dances, acrobatics, and trick plays to sold-out stadiums across the U.S.

item.title: London cabbies not ready to hand over their keys to AI-powered, autonomous taxis
item.description: London black cab drivers, who are required to memorize thousands of streets to get their license, are being tested in a new way. Several companies are trying to bring robotaxis to the city's streets.

item.title: 7/5/2026: Elemental Crisis; The Knowledge; Banana Ball
item.description: First, a report on the only active rare earth mine in the U.S. Then, London's cabbies skeptical about robotaxis. And, a look inside the world of Banana Ball.

item.title: 7/5: CBS Weekend News
item.description: Folarin Balogun will play in U.S. World Cup match against Belgium; extreme weather mars Fourth of July weekend.

item.title: New evacuation orders after "significant growth" of Willow Fire
item.description: Authorities overseeing the firefight against the week-old Willow Fire west of Leadville issued new evacuation and pre-evacuation orders Sunday morning after the blaze grew by nearly 1,000 acres in 24 hours.

item.title: D.C. air quality drops to "very unhealthy" levels after July 4th fireworks
item.description: A "purple" air quality alert was issued for Washington, D.C., and surrounding areas, meaning pollution reached levels considered "very unhealthy."

item.title: DNA technology leads to identification of Revolutionary War soldier
item.description: The remains of a Revolutionary War soldier were identified as a young man from Maryland just before America's 250th anniversary.
